---
title: "ARG_3.0"
format: html
---
## setup chunks
```{r setup}
#| message: false
#| warning: false

library(tidyverse)
library(DESeq2)
library(apeglm)
library(phyloseq)
library(microbiome)
library(vegan)
library(arrow)
library(VennDiagram)
library(pheatmap)
library(ggrepel)
library(here)
library(grid)
library(rstatix)
library(ggpubr)

# Set figure output directory
if (!dir.exists("../figures")) dir.create("../figures", recursive = TRUE)
if (!dir.exists("../rgiresults")) dir.create("../rgiresults", recursive = TRUE)
```
### color codes and themes
```{r - set figure template}
#| include = FALSE

# Global text size used for ggplot figures in this report
FIG_BASE_SIZE <- 18
PVAL_LABEL_SIZE <- 5

format_p_label <- function(p) {
  dplyr::case_when(
    is.na(p) ~ NA_character_,
    p < 0.001 ~ "p<0.001",
    TRUE ~ paste0("p=", sprintf("%.3f", p))
  )
}

stat_pvalue_manual <- function(data, ..., p_col = NULL, label = NULL, hide.ns = TRUE, size = PVAL_LABEL_SIZE, alpha = 0.05) {
  if (!is.data.frame(data)) {
    return(ggpubr::stat_pvalue_manual(data = data, ..., label = label, hide.ns = hide.ns, size = size))
  }

  # Prefer adjusted p-values when available.
  p_col_candidates <- unique(c(p_col, "p.adj", "p.value", "p"))
  p_col_detected <- p_col_candidates[p_col_candidates %in% names(data)][1]

  if (!is.na(p_col_detected)) {
    data <- data %>%
      dplyr::filter(!is.na(.data[[p_col_detected]]), .data[[p_col_detected]] < alpha)

    # Keep explicit label columns (for example: "label" or "p.adj.formatted").
    use_global_label <- is.null(label) || identical(label, p_col_detected) || label %in% c("p", "p.adj", "p.value")

    if (!identical(label, "p.signif") && use_global_label) {
      data <- data %>%
        dplyr::mutate(.p_label_global = format_p_label(.data[[p_col_detected]]))
      label <- ".p_label_global"
    }
  }

  if (nrow(data) == 0) {
    return(ggplot2::geom_blank())
  }

  ggpubr::stat_pvalue_manual(data = data, ..., label = label, hide.ns = hide.ns, size = size)
}

my_theme <- function() {
  theme_bw(base_family = "DejaVu Sans", 
           base_size = FIG_BASE_SIZE) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
      axis.title = element_text(size = rel(1.05)),
      axis.text = element_text(size = rel(0.95)),
      legend.title = element_text(size = rel(0.95)),
      legend.text = element_text(size = rel(0.9)),
      strip.text = element_text(size = rel(0.95))
    )
}

library(ggplot2)
theme_set(my_theme())

# Load color codes from YAML file
color_codes <- yaml::read_yaml("../../Metadata/color_codes.yaml")

# Extract specific color palettes
sample_type_colors <- unlist(color_codes$sample_types)
pig_stage_colors <- unlist(color_codes$pig_stages)
category2_colors <- unlist(color_codes$category2)

# publication theme
theme_publication <- function() {
  my_theme()+
    theme(
      legend.position = "none"
    )}

theme_publication_with_title <- function(){
  theme_publication() +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = FIG_BASE_SIZE + 2),
          plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = FIG_BASE_SIZE))
}
```

## load data

```{r}


# Load feature info and metadata

counts_raw <- read_feather("../data/rgi_combined/All_Mapped_Reads.feather") # loading raw counts

# store as csv
write.csv(counts_raw, "../data/rgi_combined/All_Mapped_Reads.csv", row.names = FALSE)


Depth_raw <- read_feather("../data/rgi_combined/Depth.feather") # loading relative abundance (Depth)

feats <- read_feather("../data/rgi_combined/feature_info.feather") # loading feature info

# Manual curation for AAC(6')-Ib-cr variants:
# 1) ensure Aminoglycosides is present
# 2) remove fluoroquinolone/quinolone antibiotic labels to avoid class splitting
aac_ib_cr_terms <- c(
  "AAC(6')-Ib-cr1",
  "AAC(6')-Ib-cr3",
  "AAC(6')-Ib-cr5",
  "AAC(6')-Ib-cr6",
  "AAC(6')-Ib-cr7"
)

feats <- feats %>%
  mutate(
    ARO_Term = as.character(ARO_Term),
    Drug_Class = as.character(Drug_Class),
    Drug_Class = case_when(
      ARO_Term %in% aac_ib_cr_terms &
        !str_detect(coalesce(Drug_Class, ""), "(^|;\\s*)Aminoglycosides($|;\\s*)") ~
        str_trim(paste0(coalesce(Drug_Class, ""), if_else(coalesce(Drug_Class, "") == "", "", "; "), "Aminoglycosides")),
      TRUE ~ Drug_Class
    ),
    Drug_Class = case_when(
      ARO_Term %in% aac_ib_cr_terms ~ Drug_Class %>%
        str_remove_all("(?i)(^|;\\s*)(fluoroquinolone antibiotic|quinolone antibiotic)(?=;|$)") %>%
        str_replace_all("\\s*;\\s*", "; ") %>%
        str_replace_all("^;\\s*|\\s*;$", "") %>%
        na_if(""),
      TRUE ~ Drug_Class
    )
  )

feats <- feats %>%
  mutate(Prevalence_Sequence_ID = as.character(Prevalence_Sequence_ID)) %>%
  distinct(Prevalence_Sequence_ID, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  column_to_rownames(var = "Prevalence_Sequence_ID")

meta <- read_csv("../../Metadata/metadata_silas.csv") %>% # loading metadata
        filter(Duplicated_Sample == FALSE & id != "s24H1")%>%
        column_to_rownames("id")

table(meta$category)

read_stat <- read_tsv("../data/rgi_map_results/bam_stats.tsv")%>%
  column_to_rownames("sample") # loading read stats (for mapping rate)

# transpose matrix in correct shape (genes as rows and samples as columns)

sample_names <- counts_raw[[1]]

mat <- as.matrix(counts_raw[,-1, drop = FALSE])

mode(mat) <- "numeric" 
rownames(mat) <- sample_names

counts_df <- as.data.frame(t(mat), check.names = FALSE)

# remove ppB-samples
counts_df <- counts_df[, !grepl("ppB", colnames(counts_df))]


# check difference between meta and counts samples
names_meta <- rownames(meta)
names_counts <- colnames(counts_df)

diff_names <- setdiff(names_meta, names_counts)
print(diff_names)

names_counts_raw <- colnames(counts_raw)
if ("s24H1" %in% colnames(counts_raw)) { print("yes")} else {print("no")}


# check All_Mapped_Reads columns
library(arrow)
df <- read_feather("../data/rgi_combined/All_Mapped_Reads.feather")
"s24H1" %in% colnames(df)



```
#### inspect data
```{r}
# genes
cat("Numbers of genes:", ncol(counts_raw), "\n")

cat("Numbers of ARO-Terms:", length(unique(feats$ARO_Term)), "\n")

# cat("Numbers of Drug Classes:", length(unique(cpm_drugclass_long$Drug_Class)), "\n")

res_mech <- feats %>%
  pull(Resistance_Mechanism) %>%
  unique()


print(res_mech)
cat("Numbers of Resistance Mechanisms:", length(unique(feats$Resistance_Mechanism)), "\n")
```


### library size

```{r}
sample_depths <- colSums(counts_df)
sort(sample_depths) %>% head(10)  # check the lowest samples

# visualize
data.frame(
  sample = names(sample_depths),
  depth  = sample_depths
) %>%
  left_join(meta %>% rownames_to_column("sample"), by = "sample") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(sample, depth), y = depth, fill = category)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = min(sample_depths), color = "red", linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = sample_type_colors) +
  labs(title = "ARG library depth per sample",
       x = "Sample", y = "Total ARG reads") +
  theme_publication() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, size = 6))

#ggsave
ggsave("../figures/ARG_library_depth_per_sample.svg", width = 10, height = 6, web_fonts = "DejaVu Sans")

## boxplot
data.frame(
  sample = names(sample_depths),
  depth  = sample_depths
) %>%
  left_join(meta %>% rownames_to_column("sample"), by = "sample") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = category, y = depth, fill = category)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_jitter(alpha = 0.5, width = 0.1) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = sample_type_colors) +
  labs(title = "ARG library depth by category",
       x = "Category", y = "Total ARG reads") +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))+
  theme_publication()

## ggsave
ggsave("../figures/ARG_library_depth_by_category.svg", width = 8, height = 6, web_fonts = "DejaVu Sans")
```

### Normalize counts to relative abundance (Depth) for diversity analyses

We use the following formula: $$ \text{norm coverage} = \frac{\text{N mapped reads}}{L - r + 1} $$ - N = number of reads mapped to the gene (from counts) - L = gene length (from feats) - r = average read length (from read_stat)

```{r}
# make reference length numeric (handle list columns from feather)
feats$Reference_Length <- as.numeric(feats$Reference_Length)

# Align to counts
feats_aligned <- feats[rownames(counts_df), , drop = FALSE]

# Aling read_stats
read_stat_aligned <- read_stat[colnames(counts_df), , drop = FALSE]

# Directly pull the numeric vector
L <- as.numeric(feats_aligned$Reference_Length)


counts_mat <- as.matrix(counts_df)

length(L); sum(is.na(L)) # should be n_genes and 0

# read lengths aligned to the count matrix columns
r <- as.numeric(read_stat_aligned$avg_mapped_length)

bad_r_idx <- which(is.na(r))
length(bad_r_idx)
colnames(counts_df)[bad_r_idx]
setdiff(colnames(counts_df), rownames(read_stat))
read_stat_aligned[bad_r_idx, "avg_mapped_length", drop = FALSE]

# fail early if lengths are missing
if (anyNA(L) || anyNA(r)) {
  stop("Missing gene or read lengths after alignment. Check read_stat and feats rownames.")
}

# build denominator matrix: genes x samples
denom <- outer(L, r, FUN = function(Li, rj) Li - rj + 1)
denom[denom < 1] <- 1   # avoid 0 or negative

# Sanity check
cat("Denom range: [", min(denom), ",", max(denom), "]\n")
cat("Any zeros:", any(denom == 0), "\n")


# normalized depth: reads per possible start site effective-length corrected
counts_norm <- counts_mat / denom


### --- normalize against ARG-mapping reads ---
# counts per millions
cpm <- sweep(counts_norm, MARGIN = 2, STATS = colSums(counts_norm), FUN = "/") * 1e6


# sanity check if col sums sum up to 1e6 before filtering
stopifnot(all(abs(colSums(cpm) - 1e6) < 1e-6))
cat("✓ cpm columns sum to 1e6 before filter: OK\n")

# Global CPM filter: set detections below 1 CPM to zero
cpm_unfiltered <- cpm
n_below_1 <- sum(cpm < 1, na.rm = TRUE)
cpm[cpm < 1] <- 0
cat("Global CPM filter applied (threshold = 1). Entries set to 0:", n_below_1, "\n")


head(cpm)

```

sanity check

```{r}
boxplot(r ~ meta$category)
```

### Phyloseq-Object

```{r}
# Phyloseq with RAW COUNTS
otu_raw <- otu_table(as.matrix(counts_df), taxa_are_rows = TRUE)
sam_data <- sample_data(meta)
physeq_raw <- phyloseq(otu_raw, sam_data)


# Phyloseq with normalized Depth
otu_norm <- otu_table(as.matrix(cpm), taxa_are_rows = TRUE)
physeq_norm <- phyloseq(otu_norm, sam_data)

# save phyesq_norm
saveRDS(physeq_norm, "../results/physeq_normalized.rds")
```

### Calculate mapping rate to CARD database

wie viel Reads mappend zu der card database im vergleich zu den total reads per sample?

```{r}
mapping_stats <- read_stat %>%
  rownames_to_column("sample") %>%
  right_join(meta %>% rownames_to_column("sample"), by = "sample") %>%
  mutate(mapping_rate = mapped / total_filtered)

# Summary by category
mapping_summary <- mapping_stats %>%
  group_by(category) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_mapping_rate = mean(mapping_rate, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_mapping_rate = sd(mapping_rate, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_mapping_rate = median(mapping_rate, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

cat("=== Mapping Statistics ===\n")
cat("Reads mapped to CARD database by sample category:\n")
print(mapping_summary) 

# add statistics: Kruskal-Wallis test
mapping_stats$category <- as.factor(mapping_stats$category)
mapping_kw <- mapping_stats %>%
  rstatix::kruskal_test(mapping_rate ~ category)
cat("Kruskal-Wallis test for mapping rate differences across categories:\n")
print(mapping_kw)

# pairwise Wilcoxon tests with BH correction
mapping_pairwise <- mapping_stats %>%
  pairwise_wilcox_test(mapping_rate ~ category, p.adjust.method = "BH") %>%
  add_significance()


# add y-position for plotting
mapping_pairwise <- mapping_pairwise %>%
  mutate(
    y.position = seq(
      from = max(mapping_stats$mapping_rate, na.rm = TRUE) * 1.05,
      to   = max(mapping_stats$mapping_rate, na.rm = TRUE) * 1.25,
      length.out = n()
    )
  )%>%
  ungroup()

# Plot mapping rate by category
p_mapping <- ggplot(mapping_stats, aes(x = category, y = mapping_rate, fill = category)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_jitter(alpha = 0.5, width = 0.1) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = sample_type_colors) +
  my_theme() +
  labs(
    title = "CARD Database Mapping Rate",
    subtitle = "Percentage of reads mapped to CARD antibiotic resistance genes",
    x = "",
    y = "Mapping Rate"
  ) +
  theme(legend.position = "none")+
  stat_pvalue_manual(
    mapping_pairwise,
    label = "p.adj",
    tip.length = 0.01,
    size = 5,
    family = "DejaVu Sans"
  )+
  theme_publication()+
  scale_x_discrete(labels = color_codes$label_mapping)

print(p_mapping)

ggsave("../figures/mapping_rate_to_CARD.svg", p_mapping, width = 8, height = 6, web_fonts = "DejaVu Sans")
```

## Richness & Prevlance Analysis

### Richness

```{r}
# ARG Richness (cpm > 0: presence/absence)
richness_data <- cpm
richness_data[richness_data > 0] <- 1
richness_df <- data.frame(
  sample = colnames(richness_data),
  argrichness = colSums(richness_data)
) %>% 
  right_join(meta %>% rownames_to_column("sample"), by = "sample")


# Global Kruskal-Wallis test

# richness as factor
richness_df$category <- as.factor(richness_df$category)

# global Kurskal-Wallis test
stats_richness <- richness_df %>%
  rstatix::kruskal_test(argrichness ~ category)

stats_richness
cat("=== The global test has a significance level of p =", stats_richness$p, "indicating that there are significant differences in ARG richness across the categories.\n")

# pairwise Wilcoxon tests with Benjamini-Hochberg correction
pairwise_richness <- richness_df %>%
  pairwise_wilcox_test(argrichness ~ category, p.adjust.method = "BH")%>%
  add_significance() %>%
  mutate(
    # define label position per comparison
    y.position = seq(
      from = max(richness_df$argrichness, na.rm = T) * 1.05,
      to = max(richness_df$argrichness, na.rm =T)*1.25,
      length.out = n()
      )
    )



# Plot richness
p_rich <- ggplot(richness_df, aes(x = category, y = argrichness, fill = category)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_jitter(alpha = 0.5, width = 0.1) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = color_codes$sample_types) +
  my_theme()+
  labs(
    title = "ARG Richness by Category")+
  ylab("Richness")+
  xlab("")+
  stat_pvalue_manual(
    pairwise_richness,
    label = "p.adj",
    tip.length = 0.01,
    size = 4,
    family = "DejaVu Sans"
  )+
  theme_publication()
print(p_rich)

```

save this plot

```{r}
ggsave("../figures/ARG_richness_by_category.svg", p_rich, width = 8, height = 6, web_fonts = "DejaVu Sans")
```

### sensitivity 

```{r}
# Sensitivity of richness to CPM abundance/prevalence filters.
# x = minimum CPM per sample for presence; y = minimum prevalence across all samples.
min_cpm_grid <- c(0.1, 1, 5)
min_prev_grid <- c(0.01, 0.05, 0.10, 0.20)

sensitivity_tbl <- purrr::map_dfr(min_cpm_grid, function(min_cpm_i) {
  # Presence/absence under current CPM threshold.
  pa_mat <- (cpm >= min_cpm_i) * 1

  purrr::map_dfr(min_prev_grid, function(min_prev_i) {
    keep_features <- rowMeans(pa_mat, na.rm = TRUE) >= min_prev_i

    pa_filt <- pa_mat[keep_features, , drop = FALSE]
    richness_i <- colSums(pa_filt, na.rm = TRUE)

    richness_df_i <- tibble(
      sample = names(richness_i),
      argrichness = as.numeric(richness_i)
    ) %>%
      left_join(meta %>% rownames_to_column("sample"), by = "sample") %>%
      left_join(
        data.frame(sample = rownames(read_stat), depth = read_stat$total_filtered),
        by = "sample"
      ) %>%
      filter(!is.na(category), !is.na(depth))

    lm_i <- lm(argrichness ~ category + depth, data = richness_df_i)
    anova_i <- anova(lm_i)

    tibble(
      min_cpm = min_cpm_i,
      min_prevalence = min_prev_i,
      n_features_kept = sum(keep_features),
      median_richness = median(richness_df_i$argrichness, na.rm = TRUE),
      p_category_depth_adjusted = anova_i["category", "Pr(>F)"]
    )
  })
}) %>%
  mutate(
    min_prevalence_pct = paste0(min_prevalence * 100, "%"),
    signif = case_when(
      is.na(p_category_depth_adjusted) ~ "NA",
      p_category_depth_adjusted < 0.001 ~ "***",
      p_category_depth_adjusted < 0.01 ~ "**",
      p_category_depth_adjusted < 0.05 ~ "*",
      TRUE ~ "ns"
    )
  )

print(sensitivity_tbl)
write_csv(sensitivity_tbl, "../results/richness_sensitivity_cpm_prevalence.csv")

p_sensitivity <- ggplot(
  sensitivity_tbl,
  aes(x = factor(min_cpm), y = min_prevalence_pct, fill = median_richness)
) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.3) +
  geom_text(aes(label = signif), size = 4, family = "DejaVu Sans") +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "C", name = "Median richness") +
  labs(
    title = "Richness sensitivity to CPM/prevalence filters",
    subtitle = "Stars show depth-adjusted category effect (lm: richness ~ category + depth)",
    x = "Minimum CPM threshold (x)",
    y = "Minimum prevalence threshold (y)"
  ) +
  my_theme()

print(p_sensitivity)
ggsave(
  "../figures/richness_sensitivity_cpm_prevalence.svg",
  p_sensitivity,
  width = 8,
  height = 5,
  web_fonts = "DejaVu Sans"
)
```

#### zero-check in controls

```{r}
# Check whether controls are dominated by zeros in CPM profiles.
sample_zero_tbl <- tibble(
  sample = colnames(cpm),
  zero_fraction = colMeans(cpm == 0, na.rm = TRUE),
  nonzero_fraction = colMeans(cpm > 0, na.rm = TRUE)
) %>%
  left_join(meta %>% rownames_to_column("sample") %>% select(sample, category), by = "sample")

control_level <- "Non_farmer_controls"

controls_zero_tbl <- sample_zero_tbl %>%
  filter(category == control_level)

cat("Control samples:", nrow(controls_zero_tbl), "\n")
cat("Mean zero fraction in controls:", round(mean(controls_zero_tbl$zero_fraction, na.rm = TRUE), 4), "\n")
cat("Median zero fraction in controls:", round(median(controls_zero_tbl$zero_fraction, na.rm = TRUE), 4), "\n")
cat("Range zero fraction in controls:",
    round(min(controls_zero_tbl$zero_fraction, na.rm = TRUE), 4), "-",
    round(max(controls_zero_tbl$zero_fraction, na.rm = TRUE), 4), "\n")

# Optional context: controls vs other categories.
zero_by_category <- sample_zero_tbl %>%
  group_by(category) %>%
  summarise(
    n_samples = n(),
    mean_zero_fraction = mean(zero_fraction, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_zero_fraction = median(zero_fraction, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

print(zero_by_category)

# Pairwise Wilcoxon tests on sample-level zero fractions (BH-adjusted p-values).
zero_wilcox <- sample_zero_tbl %>%
  filter(!is.na(category)) %>%
  pairwise_wilcox_test(zero_fraction ~ category, p.adjust.method = "BH") %>%
  add_significance() %>%
  mutate(
    y.position = seq(
      from = max(sample_zero_tbl$zero_fraction, na.rm = TRUE) * 1.03,
      to = max(sample_zero_tbl$zero_fraction, na.rm = TRUE) * 1.12,
      length.out = n()
    )
  )

print(zero_wilcox)

zero_plot <- ggplot(sample_zero_tbl, aes(x = category, y = zero_fraction, fill = category)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.75, outlier.shape = NA) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.12, alpha = 0.45, size = 1.4) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = sample_type_colors) +
  labs(
    title = "Fraction of zero CPM entries per sample",
    subtitle = "Higher values indicate more sparse sample profiles",
    x = "",
    y = "Zero fraction"
  ) +
  stat_pvalue_manual(
    zero_wilcox,
    label = "p.adj",
    tip.length = 0.01,
    size = 3.5,
    family = "DejaVu Sans"
  ) +
  my_theme() +
  theme_publication()

print(zero_plot)

# Feature-level check inside controls: how often each feature is zero across control samples.
control_samples <- rownames(meta)[meta$category == control_level]
control_samples <- intersect(control_samples, colnames(cpm))

feature_zero_controls <- tibble(
  feature = rownames(cpm),
  zero_prevalence_controls = rowMeans(cpm[, control_samples, drop = FALSE] == 0, na.rm = TRUE)
)

cat("Features always zero in controls:",
    sum(feature_zero_controls$zero_prevalence_controls == 1),
    "of", nrow(feature_zero_controls), "\n")

write_csv(sample_zero_tbl, "../results/sample_zero_fraction_by_category.csv")
write_csv(zero_wilcox, "../results/sample_zero_fraction_pairwise_wilcox_BH.csv")
write_csv(feature_zero_controls, "../results/feature_zero_prevalence_in_controls.csv")

ggsave(
  "../figures/sample_zero_fraction_by_category.svg",
  zero_plot,
  width = 8,
  height = 5,
  web_fonts = "DejaVu Sans"
)
```



### is richness correlated with depth ?

```{r}
# Is richness correlated with depth?
richness_df <- richness_df %>%
  left_join(
    data.frame(sample = rownames(read_stat), depth = read_stat$total_filtered),
    by = "sample"
  )

cor.test(richness_df$argrichness, richness_df$depth, method = "spearman")

# Visualize
ggplot(richness_df, aes(x = depth, y = argrichness, color = category)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  scale_color_manual(values = sample_type_colors) +
  scale_x_log10() +
  my_theme() +
  labs(title = "ARG Richness vs Sequencing Depth",
       x = "Total reads (log scale)", y = "ARG Richness")

# ggsave
ggsave("../figures/ARG_richness_vs_depth.svg", width = 8, height = 6, web_fonts = "DejaVu Sans")
```

#### richness depth corrected

```{r}
library(coin)  # or use a linear model approach
library(car)
library(emmeans)

# Linear model: richness ~ category + depth
lm_richness <- lm(argrichness ~ category + depth, data = richness_df)
summary(lm_richness)

# Are category effects still significant after controlling for depth? - global F-test
anova(lm_richness)


# estimated marginal means (adjusted for depth)
emm <- emmeans(lm_richness, ~ category)
emm

# pairwise post-hoc tests
posthoc <- pairs(emm, adjust = "tukey") # Tukey-adjusted p-values

print(posthoc)


# p-values df of posthoc test
posthoc_df <- as.data.frame(posthoc) %>%
  mutate(
    p.signif = ifelse(p.value < 0.001, "***",
               ifelse(p.value < 0.01,  "**",
               ifelse(p.value < 0.05,  "*", "ns")))
  )



# Residuals nur für Visualisierung
richness_df$richness_resid <- residuals(lm(argrichness ~ depth, data = richness_df))

# Annotationen manuell erstellen
annotations <- posthoc_df %>%
  separate(contrast, into = c("group1", "group2"), sep = " - ") %>%
  mutate(
    group1 = trimws(group1),
    group2 = trimws(group2),
    y.position = c(
      max(richness_df$argrichness, na.rm = TRUE) * 1.10,
      max(richness_df$argrichness, na.rm = TRUE) * 1.20,
      max(richness_df$argrichness, na.rm = TRUE) * 1.30
    )
  )

annotations <- annotations %>%
  mutate(label = ifelse(p.value < 0.001, 
                        "p < 0.001",
                        paste0("p = ", round(p.value, 3))))

# Plot
p_rich_corrected <- ggplot(richness_df, 
                           aes(x = category, y = argrichness, fill = category)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_jitter(alpha = 0.5, width = 0.1) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = sample_type_colors) +
  my_theme() +
  labs(
    title    = "ARG Richness by Category",
    subtitle = "p-values from lm(richness ~ category + depth), Tukey-adjusted",
    x = "",
    y = "ARG Richness"
  ) +
  theme(legend.position = "none") +
  stat_pvalue_manual(
    annotations,
    label      = "label",  # exact p-values
    tip.length = 0.01,
    size       = 3.5,
    family = "DejaVu Sans"
    
  )+
  theme_publication()

print(p_rich_corrected)

# ggsave
ggsave("../figures/ARG_richness_by_category_depth_corrected.svg", p_rich_corrected, width = 8, height = 6, web_fonts = "DejaVu Sans")


## richness depth corrected per growth stage 



df_richness_stage <- richness_df %>%
  filter(!is.na(growth_stage))

# add statistics
# Linear model: richness ~ growth_stage + depth
lm_richness_stage <- lm(argrichness ~ growth_stage + depth, data = df_richness_stage)
summary(lm_richness_stage)

# Are growth_stage effects still significant after controlling for depth? - global F-test
anova(lm_richness_stage)


# estimated marginal means (adjusted for depth)
emm_stage <- emmeans(lm_richness_stage, ~ growth_stage)
emm_stage
# pairwise post-hoc tests
posthoc_stage <- pairs(emm_stage, adjust = "tukey") # Tukey
posthoc_stage

# p-values df of posthoc test
posthoc_stage_df <- as.data.frame(posthoc_stage) %>%
  mutate(
    p.signif = ifelse(p.value < 0.001, "***",
               ifelse(p.value < 0.01,  "**",
               ifelse(p.value < 0.05,  "*", "ns")))
  ) %>%
  separate(contrast, into = c("group1", "group2"), sep = " - ") %>%
  mutate(
    group1 = trimws(group1),
    group2 = trimws(group2),
    y.position = c(
      max(df_richness_stage$argrichness, na.rm = TRUE) * 1.10,
      max(df_richness_stage$argrichness, na.rm = TRUE) * 1.20,
      max(df_richness_stage$argrichness, na.rm = TRUE) * 1.30
    ),
    label = ifelse(p.value < 0.001, 
                   "p < 0.001",
                   paste0("p = ", round(p.value, 3)))
  )

# order factor to suckling -> weaning -> fattening
df_richness_stage$growth_stage <- factor(df_richness_stage$growth_stage,
                                         levels = c("Suckling", "Weaning", "Fattening"))


p_rich_corrected_stage <- ggplot(df_richness_stage, 
                           aes(x = growth_stage, y = argrichness, fill = growth_stage)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_jitter(alpha = 0.5, width = 0.1) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pig_stage_colors) +
  my_theme() +
  labs(
    title    = "ARG Richness by Category",
    subtitle = "p-values from lm(richness ~ category + depth), Tukey-adjusted",
    x = "",
    y = "ARG Richness"
  ) +
  theme_publication()+
  stat_pvalue_manual(
    posthoc_stage_df,
    label      = "label",  # significance stars
    tip.length = 0.01,
    size       = 3.5,
    family = "DejaVu Sans"
  )

print(p_rich_corrected_stage)

# ggsave
ggsave("../figures/ARG_richness_by_stage_depth_corrected.svg", p_rich_corrected_stage, width = 8, height = 6, web_fonts = "DejaVu Sans")


```

### Observed
```{r}
observed <- estimate_richness(physeq_raw, measures = "Observed") %>%
  rownames_to_column("sample") %>%
  left_join(meta %>% rownames_to_column("sample"), by = "sample") %>%
  mutate(depth = sample_sums(physeq_norm)[sample])

library(rstatix)
# add statistics for observed richness
observed$category <- as.factor(observed$category)
stats_observed <- observed %>%
  rstatix::kruskal_test(Observed ~ category)

stats_observed
cat("=== The global test for Observed richness has a significance level of p =", stats_observed$p)

# pairwise Wilcoxon tests with Benjamini-Hochberg correction

pairwise_observed <- observed %>%
  pairwise_wilcox_test(Observed ~ category, p.adjust.method = "BH")%>%
  add_significance() %>%
  mutate(
    y.position = seq(
      from = max(observed$Observed, na.rm = TRUE) * 1.05,
      to = max(observed$Observed, na.rm = TRUE) * 1.25,
      length.out = n()
    )
  )

p_observed <- ggplot(data = observed, aes(x = category, y = Observed, fill = category)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_jitter(alpha = 0.5, width = 0.1) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = sample_type_colors) +
  labs(title = "Observed Richness by Category",
       x = "", y = "Observed Richness") +
  my_theme()+
  theme(legend.position = "none") +
  stat_pvalue_manual(
    pairwise_observed,
    label = "p.adj",
    tip.length = 0.01,
    size = 4,
    family = "DejaVu Sans"
  )


print(p_observed)

```


### Prevalence

```{r}
# Prevalence Venn diagram (1% threshold)

cat("counts_df dims:", nrow(counts_df), "genes x", ncol(counts_df), "samples\n")
prevalence <- counts_df %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ .x > 0)) %>%
  select(where(is.logical)) %>%
  rownames_to_column("feature") %>%                      # features (genes/taxa)
  pivot_longer(-feature, names_to = "sample", values_to = "present") %>%
  left_join(meta %>% rownames_to_column("sample"), by = "sample") %>%
  group_by(feature, category) %>%
  summarise(prevalence = mean(present, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = category, values_from = prevalence) %>%
  column_to_rownames("feature")

head(prevalence)



# Create gene sets based on 1% prevalence threshold
gene_sets <- list(
  Pigs = rownames(prevalence)[prevalence$Pigs > 0.01],
  PigFarmers = rownames(prevalence)[prevalence$Pig_farmers > 0.01],
  Non_Farmer_Controls = rownames(prevalence)[prevalence$Non_farmer_controls > 0.01 ]
)

# Create Venn diagram
venn_plot <- venn.diagram(
  x = gene_sets,
  category.names = c("Pig", "PigFarmers", "Non_Farmer_Controls"),
  filename = NULL,
  output = TRUE,
  col = "transparent",
  fill = sample_type_colors,
  alpha = 0.50,
  cex = 1.5,
  cat.cex = 1.5,
  fontfamily = "DeJaVu Sans",
  cat.fontfamily = "DejaVu Sans",
  main.fontfamily = "DejaVu Sans"
  )
grid.newpage(); grid.draw(venn_plot)

# Save the plot
svg("../figures/Venn_Unigenes_with_AR_annotation_R.svg", width = 8, height = 6)
grid.draw(venn_plot)
dev.off()

# plot with eulerr:
library(eulerr)

fit <- euler(gene_sets)

nice_labels <- c(
  color_codes$label_mapping[["Pigs"]],
  color_codes$label_mapping[["Pig_farmers"]],
  color_codes$label_mapping[["Non_farmer_controls"]]
)

p_euler <- plot(
  fit,
  quantities = list(type = "counts", fontsize = 14),
  labels = list(labels = nice_labels,font = 2, fontsize = 14),
  fills = list(
    fill = c(
      sample_type_colors[["Pigs"]],
      sample_type_colors[["Pig_farmers"]],
      sample_type_colors[["Non_farmer_controls"]]
    ),
    alpha = 0.5
  ),
  edges = list(col = "transparent")
)

print(p_euler)

ggsave(
  "../figures/Venn_Unigenes_with_AR_annotation_euler.svg",
  p_euler,
  width = 8, height = 6
)


```

## Diversity-analysis

### alpha diversity (depth corrected)

```{r}
# Estimate alpha diversity from normalized phyloseq object ────────────────
alpha_df <- estimate_richness(physeq_norm, measures = c("Shannon", "Simpson")) %>%
  rownames_to_column("sample") %>%
  left_join(meta %>% rownames_to_column("sample"), by = "sample") %>%
  mutate(depth = sample_sums(physeq_norm)[sample])

# Add depth to alpha_df
alpha_df <- alpha_df %>%
  mutate(depth = sample_sums(physeq_norm)[sample])  # or from meta directly

# Loop over both measures
library(purrr)

alpha_models <- c("Shannon", "Simpson") %>%
  set_names() %>%
  map(~ {
    formula <- as.formula(paste(.x, "~ category + depth"))
    lm(formula, data = alpha_df)
  })

# EMMs and post-hoc per measure
alpha_emm <- map(alpha_models, ~ emmeans(.x, ~ category))
alpha_posthoc <- map(alpha_emm, ~ pairs(.x, adjust = "tukey"))

# Prepare post-hoc results for annotation
posthoc_df <- map2_dfr(alpha_posthoc, names(alpha_posthoc), ~ {
  as.data.frame(.x) %>%
    mutate(
      measure  = .y,
      p.signif = ifelse(p.value < 0.001, "***",
                 ifelse(p.value < 0.01,  "**",
                 ifelse(p.value < 0.05,  "*", "ns")))
    )
})

# separate contrast into group1 / group2
posthoc_df <- posthoc_df %>%
  separate(contrast, into = c("group1", "group2"), sep = " - ") %>%
  mutate(
    group1 = trimws(group1),
    group2 = trimws(group2)
  )

# y.position based on RAW values (not residuals!)
posthoc_df <- posthoc_df %>%
  group_by(measure) %>%
  mutate(
    y.position = seq(
      from = max(alpha_df[[unique(measure)]], na.rm = TRUE) * 1.05,  # ← raw
      to   = max(alpha_df[[unique(measure)]], na.rm = TRUE) * 1.25,
      length.out = n()
    )
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# pivot RAW values to long format
alpha_long <- alpha_df %>%
  select(sample, category, Shannon, Simpson) %>%  
  pivot_longer(
    cols      = c(Shannon, Simpson),
    names_to  = "measure",
    values_to = "value"
  )

posthoc_df$label <- scales::pvalue(posthoc_df$p.value, add_p = TRUE)

# Plot with RAW values + Tukey-adjusted p-values from lm()
p_alpha <- ggplot(alpha_long, aes(x = category, y = value, fill = category)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_jitter(alpha = 0.5, width = 0.1) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = sample_type_colors) +
  facet_wrap(~ measure, scales = "free_y") +
  stat_pvalue_manual(
    posthoc_df,
    label      = "label",
    tip.length = 0.01,
    size       = 4,
    family     = "DejaVu Sans"
  ) +
  labs(
    title    = "Alpha Diversity by Category",
    subtitle = "p-values from lm(diversity ~ category + depth), Tukey-adjusted",
    y        = "Diversity index",
    x = " "
  ) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = color_codes$label_mapping) +
  my_theme() +
  theme(
    #axis.text.x    = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 1),
    legend.position = "none"
  )+
  theme_publication()

print(p_alpha)

ggsave("../figures/alpha_diversity_ARG_counts_depth_corrected.svg", p_alpha, width = 10, height = 6, web_fonts = "DejaVu Sans")
```

### alpha diversity 5% prevalence filter

```{r}
# Sensitivity analysis: global 5% prevalence filter on physeq_norm.
# Feature is kept if CPM > 1000 in at least 5% of all samples.
otu_norm_mat <- as(otu_table(physeq_norm), "matrix")

if (!taxa_are_rows(physeq_norm)) {
  otu_norm_mat <- t(otu_norm_mat)
}

tax_prev_global <- rowMeans(otu_norm_mat > 1000, na.rm = TRUE)
keep_taxa_5pct <- names(tax_prev_global)[tax_prev_global >= 0.05]

physeq_norm_prev5 <- prune_taxa(keep_taxa_5pct, physeq_norm)

cat("Taxa kept after 5% global prevalence filter:", length(keep_taxa_5pct),
    "of", ntaxa(physeq_norm), "\n")

alpha_df_prev5 <- estimate_richness(physeq_norm_prev5, measures = c("Shannon", "Simpson")) %>%
  rownames_to_column("sample") %>%
  left_join(meta %>% rownames_to_column("sample"), by = "sample") %>%
  mutate(depth = sample_sums(physeq_norm_prev5)[sample])

alpha_models_prev5 <- c("Shannon", "Simpson") %>%
  set_names() %>%
  map(~ {
    formula <- as.formula(paste(.x, "~ category + depth"))
    lm(formula, data = alpha_df_prev5)
  })

alpha_emm_prev5 <- map(alpha_models_prev5, ~ emmeans(.x, ~ category))
alpha_posthoc_prev5 <- map(alpha_emm_prev5, ~ pairs(.x, adjust = "tukey"))

posthoc_df_prev5 <- map2_dfr(alpha_posthoc_prev5, names(alpha_posthoc_prev5), ~ {
  as.data.frame(.x) %>%
    mutate(measure = .y)
}) %>%
  separate(contrast, into = c("group1", "group2"), sep = " - ") %>%
  mutate(
    group1 = trimws(group1),
    group2 = trimws(group2)
  )

posthoc_df_prev5 <- posthoc_df_prev5 %>%
  group_by(measure) %>%
  mutate(
    y.position = seq(
      from = max(alpha_df_prev5[[unique(measure)]], na.rm = TRUE) * 1.05,
      to = max(alpha_df_prev5[[unique(measure)]], na.rm = TRUE) * 1.25,
      length.out = n()
    )
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

posthoc_df_prev5$label <- scales::pvalue(posthoc_df_prev5$p.value, add_p = TRUE)

alpha_long_prev5 <- alpha_df_prev5 %>%
  select(sample, category, Shannon, Simpson) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(Shannon, Simpson),
    names_to = "measure",
    values_to = "value"
  )

p_alpha_prev5 <- ggplot(alpha_long_prev5, aes(x = category, y = value, fill = category)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_jitter(alpha = 0.5, width = 0.1) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = sample_type_colors) +
  facet_wrap(~ measure, scales = "free_y") +
  stat_pvalue_manual(
    posthoc_df_prev5,
    label = "label",
    tip.length = 0.01,
    size = 4,
    family = "DejaVu Sans"
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Alpha Diversity by Category (5% global prevalence filter)",
    subtitle = "p-values from lm(diversity ~ category + depth), Tukey-adjusted",
    y = "Diversity index",
    x = " "
  ) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = color_codes$label_mapping) +
  my_theme() +
  theme(legend.position = "none") +
  theme_publication()

print(p_alpha_prev5)

write_csv(alpha_df_prev5, "../results/alpha_diversity_prev5_values.csv")
write_csv(posthoc_df_prev5, "../results/alpha_diversity_prev5_posthoc_tukey.csv")

ggsave(
  "../figures/alpha_diversity_ARG_counts_depth_corrected_prev5.svg",
  p_alpha_prev5,
  width = 10,
  height = 6,
  web_fonts = "DejaVu Sans"
)
```


### beta diversity

```{r}

cpm_clr <- t(microbiome::transform(cpm, "clr"))   # samples × genes ✓

pca_res <- prcomp(cpm_clr)

df_pca <- as.data.frame(pca_res$x) %>% 
  rownames_to_column("sample") %>%  # rownames ARE samples
  left_join(meta %>% rownames_to_column("sample"), by = "sample")  # Now matches!

# === Statistics ===
dist_clr <- dist(cpm_clr, method = "euclidean")

meta_perm <- meta[rownames(cpm_clr), , drop = F]

adon <- adonis2(dist_clr ~ category2, data = meta_perm, permutations = 999)


adon

# extract statistics

R2 <- adon$R2[1]
p_perm <- adon$`Pr(>F)`[1]

# === Plot ===
# Verify join worked
cat("Samples in PCA:", nrow(df_pca), "\n")
cat("NA category:", sum(is.na(df_pca$category2)), "\n")
print(head(df_pca))


pct_var <- round(100 * summary(pca_res)$importance[2, 1:2], 1)



p_pca <- ggplot(df_pca, aes(PC1, PC2, color = category2)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.7) +
  stat_ellipse(type = "norm", level = 0.95, linewidth = 0.7)+
  scale_color_manual(values = category2_colors)+
  my_theme()+
  labs(
  title = "PCA on normalized CLR transformed gene profiles",
    x = paste0("PC1: ", pct_var[1], "% variance"),
    y = paste0("PC2: ", pct_var[2], "% variance"),
    color = "Category"
  )+
  annotate("text", x = Inf, y = Inf, label = paste0("PERMANOVA R² = ", round(R2, 3), "\np = ", round(p_perm, 3)),
           hjust = 1.1, vjust = 1.1, size = 5, family = "DejaVu Sans")+
  theme_publication()+
  theme(legend.position = "right")
print(p_pca)
```

save this plot

```{r}
ggsave("../figures/beta_diversity_ARG_counts_PCA_cat2.svg", p_pca, width = 11, height = 8, web_fonts = "DejaVu Sans")
```

#### Bray-Curtis distance to pigs

Test whether pig farmers are closer to pigs than non-farmer controls are.

```{r}
# Samples x genes matrix for Bray-Curtis
cpm_t <- t(cpm)

# Bray-Curtis distance matrix among samples
bc_dist <- vegan::vegdist(cpm_t, method = "bray")
bc_mat <- as.matrix(bc_dist)

# Robust handling of possible farmer label names
category_levels <- unique(as.character(meta$category))
farmer_level_beta <- if ("Pig_farmers" %in% category_levels) {
  "Pig_farmers"
} else if ("Pig_farmer" %in% category_levels) {
  "Pig_farmer"
} else {
  stop("Neither 'Pig_farmers' nor 'Pig_farmer' found in meta$category")
}

pig_ids <- rownames(meta)[meta$category == "Pigs"]
farmer_ids <- rownames(meta)[meta$category == farmer_level_beta]
control_ids <- rownames(meta)[meta$category == "Non_farmer_controls"]

# Keep only samples present in the distance matrix
pig_ids <- intersect(pig_ids, rownames(bc_mat))
farmer_ids <- intersect(farmer_ids, rownames(bc_mat))
control_ids <- intersect(control_ids, rownames(bc_mat))

if (length(pig_ids) == 0 || length(farmer_ids) == 0 || length(control_ids) == 0) {
  stop("Missing samples for Bray-Curtis distance comparison. Check meta$category labels.")
}

# Per pig sample: mean distance to each comparison group
bc_to_pigs <- tibble::tibble(
  pig_sample = pig_ids,
  mean_to_farmers = purrr::map_dbl(pig_ids, ~ mean(bc_mat[.x, farmer_ids], na.rm = TRUE)),
  mean_to_controls = purrr::map_dbl(pig_ids, ~ mean(bc_mat[.x, control_ids], na.rm = TRUE))
) %>%
  left_join(
    meta %>%
      rownames_to_column("pig_sample") %>%
      select(pig_sample, growth_stage),
    by = "pig_sample"
  )

# Paired test across pig samples
bc_test <- wilcox.test(
  bc_to_pigs$mean_to_farmers,
  bc_to_pigs$mean_to_controls,
  paired = TRUE,
  exact = FALSE
)

p_label <- if (bc_test$p.value < 1e-3) {
  paste0("paired Wilcoxon p = ", format(bc_test$p.value, scientific = TRUE, digits = 2))
} else {
  paste0("paired Wilcoxon p = ", round(bc_test$p.value, 4))
}

plot_bc <- bc_to_pigs %>%
  select(pig_sample, mean_to_farmers, mean_to_controls, growth_stage) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(mean_to_farmers, mean_to_controls),
    names_to = "comparison",
    values_to = "distance"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    comparison = dplyr::recode(
      comparison,
      "mean_to_farmers" = "Pig farmers",
      "mean_to_controls" = "Non farmer controls"
    )
  )

y_max <- max(plot_bc$distance, na.rm = TRUE)

p_bc <- ggplot(plot_bc, aes(x = comparison, y = distance, fill = comparison)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.75, width = 0.55, outlier.shape = NA) +
  #geom_line(aes(group = pig_sample), colour = "grey70", alpha = 0.6) +
  #geom_point(aes(group = pig_sample), size = 1.6, alpha = 0.75, colour = "grey45") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Pig farmers" = "#E69F00", "Non farmer controls" = "#56B4E9")) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.1,alpha = 0.4)+
  labs(
    title = "Distance of pig samples to comparison groups",
    subtitle = p_label,
    x = "",
    y = "Mean Bray-Curtis distance to pigs"
  ) +
  annotate("text", x = 1.5, y = y_max * 1.04, label = p_label, family = "DejaVu Sans", size = 5) +
  my_theme() +
  theme_publication()

print(p_bc)

ggsave("../figures/bc_distance_to_pigs.svg", p_bc, width = 6, height = 6, web_fonts = "DejaVu Sans")

cat("N pig samples:", nrow(bc_to_pigs), "\n")
cat("Mean distance to pig farmers:", round(mean(bc_to_pigs$mean_to_farmers), 4), "\n")
cat("Mean distance to non farmer controls:", round(mean(bc_to_pigs$mean_to_controls), 4), "\n")
print(bc_test)
```


#### Bray-Curtis distance from pig farmers to pig stages and controls

For each pig farmer sample, compute mean distance to Suckling/Weaning/Fattening pigs and to controls.

```{r}
# Reuse sample x sample Bray-Curtis matrix from the previous chunk
# (bc_mat computed on t(cpm))

# Robust handling of possible farmer label names
category_levels <- unique(as.character(meta$category))
farmer_level_beta <- if ("Pig_farmers" %in% category_levels) {
  "Pig_farmers"
} else if ("Pig_farmer" %in% category_levels) {
  "Pig_farmer"
} else {
  stop("Neither 'Pig_farmers' nor 'Pig_farmer' found in meta$category")
}

ordered_stages <- c("Suckling", "Weaning", "Fattening")

pig_stage_tbl <- meta %>%
  rownames_to_column("sample") %>%
  filter(category == "Pigs", !is.na(growth_stage), growth_stage != "") %>%
  mutate(growth_stage = as.character(growth_stage))

present_stages <- ordered_stages[ordered_stages %in% unique(pig_stage_tbl$growth_stage)]
if (length(present_stages) < 3) {
  stop("Need all pig stages (Suckling, Weaning, Fattening) in metadata for this comparison.")
}

pig_stage_tbl <- pig_stage_tbl %>%
  filter(growth_stage %in% ordered_stages) %>%
  mutate(growth_stage = factor(growth_stage, levels = ordered_stages))

stage_map <- split(pig_stage_tbl$sample, pig_stage_tbl$growth_stage)
stage_map <- lapply(stage_map, intersect, y = rownames(bc_mat))

farmer_ids <- rownames(meta)[meta$category == farmer_level_beta]
control_ids <- rownames(meta)[meta$category == "Non_farmer_controls"]
pig_ids <- rownames(meta)[meta$category == "Pigs"]

farmer_ids <- intersect(farmer_ids, rownames(bc_mat))
control_ids <- intersect(control_ids, rownames(bc_mat))
pig_ids <- intersect(pig_ids, rownames(bc_mat))

if (length(farmer_ids) == 0 || length(control_ids) == 0 || length(pig_ids) == 0) {
  stop("Missing farmer/control/pig samples in Bray-Curtis matrix.")
}

# One row per pig-farmer sample with mean distance to each reference group
bc_farmer_df <- tibble::tibble(
  farmer_sample = farmer_ids,
  to_suckling = purrr::map_dbl(farmer_ids, ~ mean(bc_mat[.x, stage_map[["Suckling"]]], na.rm = TRUE)),
  to_weaning = purrr::map_dbl(farmer_ids, ~ mean(bc_mat[.x, stage_map[["Weaning"]]], na.rm = TRUE)),
  to_fattening = purrr::map_dbl(farmer_ids, ~ mean(bc_mat[.x, stage_map[["Fattening"]]], na.rm = TRUE)),
  to_controls = purrr::map_dbl(farmer_ids, ~ mean(bc_mat[.x, control_ids], na.rm = TRUE)),
  to_all_pigs = purrr::map_dbl(farmer_ids, ~ mean(bc_mat[.x, pig_ids], na.rm = TRUE))
)

plot_df <- bc_farmer_df %>%
  select(farmer_sample, to_suckling, to_weaning, to_fattening, to_controls) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(to_suckling, to_weaning, to_fattening, to_controls),
    names_to = "comparison",
    values_to = "distance"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    comparison = factor(
      comparison,
      levels = c("to_suckling", "to_weaning", "to_fattening", "to_controls"),
      labels = c("Suckling pigs", "Weaning pigs", "Fattening pigs", "Controls")
    )
  )

# Paired tests: each farmer sample against controls baseline
stage_vs_ctrl_tests <- tibble::tibble(
  stage = c("Suckling", "Weaning", "Fattening"),
  p.value = c(
    wilcox.test(bc_farmer_df$to_suckling, bc_farmer_df$to_controls, paired = TRUE, exact = FALSE)$p.value,
    wilcox.test(bc_farmer_df$to_weaning, bc_farmer_df$to_controls, paired = TRUE, exact = FALSE)$p.value,
    wilcox.test(bc_farmer_df$to_fattening, bc_farmer_df$to_controls, paired = TRUE, exact = FALSE)$p.value
  )
) %>%
  mutate(p.adj = p.adjust(p.value, method = "BH"))

# Overall question: are farmers generally closer to pigs than to controls?
overall_test <- wilcox.test(
  bc_farmer_df$to_all_pigs,
  bc_farmer_df$to_controls,
  paired = TRUE,
  exact = FALSE
)

overall_label <- if (overall_test$p.value < 1e-3) {
  paste0("paired Wilcoxon (all pigs vs controls) p = ", format(overall_test$p.value, scientific = TRUE, digits = 2))
} else {
  paste0("paired Wilcoxon (all pigs vs controls) p = ", round(overall_test$p.value, 5))
}

stage_color_map <- c(
  "Suckling" = if ("Suckling" %in% names(pig_stage_colors)) pig_stage_colors[["Suckling"]] else "#66C2A5",
  "Weaning" = if ("Weaning" %in% names(pig_stage_colors)) pig_stage_colors[["Weaning"]] else "#FC8D62",
  "Fattening" = if ("Fattening" %in% names(pig_stage_colors)) pig_stage_colors[["Fattening"]] else "#8DA0CB"
)

fill_vals <- c(
  "Suckling pigs" = stage_color_map[["Suckling"]],
  "Weaning pigs" = stage_color_map[["Weaning"]],
  "Fattening pigs" = stage_color_map[["Fattening"]],
  "Controls" = "#56B4E9"
)

# Create annotation tibble for stage vs. control pairwise comparisons
bc_pvalue_anno <- tibble(
  group1 = c("Suckling pigs", "Weaning pigs", "Fattening pigs"),
  group2 = c("Controls", "Controls", "Controls"),
  xmin = c(1, 2, 3),  # Suckling, Weaning, Fattening positions
  xmax = c(4, 4, 4),  # Controls position
  p.adj = stage_vs_ctrl_tests$p.adj
) %>%
  mutate(
    p.adj_label = case_when(
      p.adj < 0.001 ~ "<0.001",
      TRUE ~ as.character(round(p.adj, 3))
    )
  )

# Calculate y positions based on data range
y_max <- max(plot_df$distance, na.rm = TRUE)
y_min <- min(plot_df$distance, na.rm = TRUE)
y_range <- y_max - y_min
y_step <- y_range * 0.05

# Add y positions for significance bars (stack vertically)
bc_pvalue_anno <- bc_pvalue_anno %>%
  mutate(
    y = y_max + y_step * (row_number())
  )

p_bc_stage <- ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = comparison, y = distance, fill = comparison)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.75, width = 0.6, outlier.shape = NA) +
  #geom_point(aes(group = farmer_sample), size = 1.5, alpha = 0.75, colour = "grey45") +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.15, alpha = 0.4)+
  scale_fill_manual(values = fill_vals) +
  stat_pvalue_manual(
    data = bc_pvalue_anno,
    xmin = "xmin",
    xmax = "xmax",
    y.position = "y",
    label = "p.adj_label",
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    tip.length = 0.01,
    bracket.size = 0.3,
    size = 5,
    family = "DejaVu Sans"
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Pig farmers: distance to pig stages and controls",
    subtitle = overall_label,
    x = "",
    y = "Avg. Bray–Curtis distance",
    fill = "Reference group"
  ) +
  theme_publication()

print(p_bc_stage)
ggsave("../figures/bc_distance_from_farmers_to_stages_and_controls.svg", p_bc_stage, width = 9, height = 6, web_fonts = "DejaVu Sans")

cat("N pig-farmer samples:", nrow(bc_farmer_df), "\n")

distance_summary <- bc_farmer_df %>%
  summarise(
    mean_to_suckling = mean(to_suckling, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_to_weaning = mean(to_weaning, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_to_fattening = mean(to_fattening, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_to_controls = mean(to_controls, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_to_all_pigs = mean(to_all_pigs, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

print(distance_summary)
print(stage_vs_ctrl_tests)
print(overall_test)
```

#### beta diversity - stages

here I want to make the same pca plot as above but use the stages separately - so that we have a cluster of the controls but then also a cluster of all pig farmers and pig stages

```{r}
df_pca_stage <- df_pca %>%
  mutate(
    pca_group = case_when(
      category %in% c("Pigs", "Pig_farmers") & !is.na(growth_stage) & growth_stage != "" ~ as.character(growth_stage),
      TRUE ~ as.character(category)
    )
  )

# Keep category colors for controls/farmers; use pig_stage_colors for pig stages present in data.
stage_levels <- df_pca_stage %>%
  filter(category %in% c("Pigs", "Pig_farmers")) %>%
  pull(growth_stage) %>%
  as.character() %>%
  unique() %>%
  na.omit()

stage_levels <- stage_levels[stage_levels != ""]

pca_group_levels <- c("Non_farmer_controls", "Pig_farmers", stage_levels)
df_pca_stage$pca_group <- factor(df_pca_stage$pca_group, levels = unique(pca_group_levels))

pca_group_colors <- c(
  "Non_farmer_controls" = sample_type_colors[["Non_farmer_controls"]],
  "Pig_farmers" = sample_type_colors[["Pig_farmers"]]
)

stage_color_map <- pig_stage_colors[intersect(names(pig_stage_colors), stage_levels)]
missing_stage_cols <- setdiff(stage_levels, names(stage_color_map))
if (length(missing_stage_cols) > 0) {
  extra_cols <- grDevices::hcl.colors(length(missing_stage_cols), palette = "Dark 3")
  names(extra_cols) <- missing_stage_cols
  stage_color_map <- c(stage_color_map, extra_cols)
}

# === Statistics ===
# PERMANOVA by growth stage (pig samples only, with non-missing stage labels)
meta_stage_perm <- meta_perm %>%
  rownames_to_column("sample") %>%
  filter(category == "Pigs", !is.na(growth_stage), growth_stage != "") %>%
  mutate(growth_stage = factor(growth_stage))

if (n_distinct(meta_stage_perm$growth_stage) >= 2) {
  stage_samples <- meta_stage_perm$sample
  dist_clr_stage <- as.dist(as.matrix(dist_clr)[stage_samples, stage_samples, drop = FALSE])

  adon_growth_stage <- adonis2(
    dist_clr_stage ~ growth_stage,
    data = meta_stage_perm %>% column_to_rownames("sample"),
    permutations = 999
  )

  r2_stage <- adon_growth_stage$R2[1]
  p_stage <- adon_growth_stage$`Pr(>F)`[1]
  permanova_stage_label <- paste0(
    "PERMANOVA (growth stage) R2 = ", round(r2_stage, 3),
    " | p = ", ifelse(p_stage < 0.001, "<0.001", sprintf("%.3f", p_stage))
  )

  write_csv(
    tibble(
      term = "growth_stage",
      R2 = r2_stage,
      p_value = p_stage,
      n_samples = nrow(meta_stage_perm),
      n_levels = n_distinct(meta_stage_perm$growth_stage)
    ),
    "../results/permanova_growth_stage_pigs.csv"
  )

  print(adon_growth_stage)
} else {
  permanova_stage_label <- "PERMANOVA (growth stage): not enough stage levels"
  warning("Not enough growth_stage levels among pig samples for PERMANOVA.")
}


pca_group_colors <- c(pca_group_colors, stage_color_map)

p_pca_2 <- ggplot(df_pca_stage, aes(PC1, PC2, color = pca_group, shape = category)) +
  geom_point(size = 3.2, alpha = 0.85) +
  stat_ellipse(
    data = df_pca_stage,
    aes(PC1, PC2, group = pca_group, color = pca_group),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    type = "norm",
    level = 0.95,
    linewidth = 0.7,
    alpha = 0.15
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = pca_group_colors, breaks = levels(df_pca_stage$pca_group), drop = FALSE) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c("Pigs" = 16, "Pig_farmers" = 17, "Non_farmer_controls" = 15), drop = FALSE) +
  labs(
    title = "PCA with stage-aware grouping",
    subtitle = paste(
      "Pigs are colored by growth stage; farmers and controls by category",
      permanova_stage_label,
      sep = "\n"
    ),
    x = paste0("PC1: ", pct_var[1], "% variance"),
    y = paste0("PC2: ", pct_var[2], "% variance"),
    color = "Stage / category",
    shape = "Category"
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = Inf,
    y = Inf,
    label = permanova_stage_label,
    hjust = 1.05,
    vjust = 1.5,
    size = 4,
    family = "DejaVu Sans"
  ) +
  my_theme()+
  theme_publication()+
  theme(legend.position = "left")
print(p_pca_2)

#ggsave
ggsave("../figures/PCA_growth_stage.svg", p_pca_2, width = 10, height = 6, web_fonts = "DejaVu Sans")
```

### eveness (Pielou's eveness)

```{r}

cpm_t <- t(cpm)
H <- diversity(cpm_t, index = "shannon") # shannon H
S <- specnumber(cpm_t) # richness S
J <- H / log(S) # Pielou's eveness J

# combine with metadata
eveness_df <- data.frame(sample = rownames(cpm_t), Pielou_J = J) %>%
  left_join(meta %>% rownames_to_column("sample"), by = "sample")

# pairwise Wilcoxon tests for eveness
stat_eveness <- eveness_df %>%
  pairwise_wilcox_test(Pielou_J ~ category, p.adjust.method = "BH") %>%
  add_significance() %>%
  mutate(
    y.position = seq(
      from = max(eveness_df$Pielou_J, na.rm = TRUE) * 1.05,
      to   = max(eveness_df$Pielou_J, na.rm = TRUE) * 1.25,
      length.out = n()
    )
  )

# Plot eveness
p_eveness <- ggplot(eveness_df, aes(x = category, y = Pielou_J, color = category)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_jitter(alpha = 0.5, width = 0.2) +
  scale_color_manual(values = sample_type_colors) +
  labs(
    title = "Pielou's Eveness (J)",
    x = "",
    y = "Eveness index"
  ) +
  stat_pvalue_manual(
    stat_eveness,
    label = "p.adj",
    tip.length = 0.01,
    size = 4,
    family = "DejaVu Sans"
  ) +
  my_theme() +
  theme(
    #axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    legend.position = "none"
  )+
  scale_x_discrete(labels = color_codes$label_mapping)
print(p_eveness)

```

save evenessplot

```{r}
ggsave("../figures/eveness_ARG_counts.svg", p_eveness, width = 6, height =6, web_fonts = "DejaVu Sans")
```

## ARO-Term level

### DESeq2 - aggregated on ARO-Term

#### ARO_Term-level DESeq2 (aggregated counts)

```{r}
aro_map <- feats %>%
  rownames_to_column("Prevalence_Sequence_ID") %>%
  select(Prevalence_Sequence_ID, ARO_Term = any_of(c("ARO_Term", "AROName", "AROAccession")))

counts_aro_df <- counts_df %>%
  rownames_to_column("Prevalence_Sequence_ID") %>%
  left_join(aro_map, by = "Prevalence_Sequence_ID") %>%
  mutate(ARO_Term = ifelse(is.na(ARO_Term), Prevalence_Sequence_ID, ARO_Term)) %>%
  select(-Prevalence_Sequence_ID) %>%
  group_by(ARO_Term) %>%
  summarise(across(where(is.numeric), sum, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

counts_aro_mat <- as.matrix(counts_aro_df %>% select(-ARO_Term))
rownames(counts_aro_mat) <- counts_aro_df$ARO_Term
mode(counts_aro_mat) <- "integer"

common_samples <- intersect(rownames(meta), colnames(counts_aro_mat))
meta <- meta[common_samples, , drop = FALSE]
counts_aro_mat <- counts_aro_mat[, common_samples, drop = FALSE]

category_levels <- unique(as.character(meta$category))
farmer_level <- if ("Pig_farmer" %in% category_levels) {
  "Pig_farmer"
} else if ("Pig_farmers" %in% category_levels) {
  "Pig_farmers"
} else {
  stop("Neither 'Pig_farmer' nor 'Pig_farmers' found in meta$category")
}

dds_aro <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(countData = counts_aro_mat, colData = meta, design = ~ category)
dds_aro <- estimateSizeFactors(dds_aro, type = "poscounts")
dds_aro <- DESeq(dds_aro)

res_aro_pig_vs_ctrl <- results(dds_aro, contrast = c("category", "Pigs", "Non_farmer_controls")) %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  rownames_to_column("ARO_Term")

res_aro_farmer_vs_ctrl <- results(dds_aro, contrast = c("category", farmer_level, "Non_farmer_controls")) %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  rownames_to_column("ARO_Term")

res_aro_both <- res_aro_pig_vs_ctrl %>%
  dplyr::rename(
    log2FC_PIG_CTRL = log2FoldChange,
    pvalue_PIG_CTRL = pvalue,
    padj_PIG_CTRL = padj
  ) %>%
  full_join(
    res_aro_farmer_vs_ctrl %>%
      dplyr::rename(
        log2FC_PIGFARMER_CTRL = log2FoldChange,
        pvalue_PIGFARMER_CTRL = pvalue,
        padj_PIGFARMER_CTRL = padj
      ),
    by = "ARO_Term"
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(log2FC_PIG_CTRL) & !is.na(log2FC_PIGFARMER_CTRL))

# Backward-compatible normalization in case an older in-memory object still has .x/.y names.
pick_num_col <- function(df, candidates) {
  for (nm in candidates) {
    if (nm %in% names(df)) return(as.numeric(df[[nm]]))
  }
  rep(NA_real_, nrow(df))
}

res_aro_both <- res_aro_both %>%
  mutate(
    pvalue_PIG_CTRL = pick_num_col(., c("pvalue_PIG_CTRL", "pvalue.x", "pvalue")),
    padj_PIG_CTRL = pick_num_col(., c("padj_PIG_CTRL", "padj.x", "padj")),
    pvalue_PIGFARMER_CTRL = pick_num_col(., c("pvalue_PIGFARMER_CTRL", "pvalue.y")),
    padj_PIGFARMER_CTRL = pick_num_col(., c("padj_PIGFARMER_CTRL", "padj.y"))
  )

# add labels of top 20 (top right) ARO_Terms
top20_aro_terms <- res_aro_both %>%
  filter(log2FC_PIG_CTRL > 1, log2FC_PIGFARMER_CTRL > 1) %>%
  arrange(desc(log2FC_PIG_CTRL), desc(log2FC_PIGFARMER_CTRL)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 20) %>%
  pull(ARO_Term)

# Export top 20 annotated ARO terms with DESeq2 statistics from both models.
aro_annotation_tbl <- feats %>%
  rownames_to_column("Prevalence_Sequence_ID") %>%
  transmute(
    ARO_Term = if_else(
      is.na(ARO_Term) | ARO_Term == "",
      as.character(Prevalence_Sequence_ID),
      as.character(ARO_Term)
    ),
    Drug_Class = as.character(Drug_Class),
    Resistance_Mechanism = as.character(Resistance_Mechanism)
  ) %>%
  group_by(ARO_Term) %>%
  summarise(
    Drug_Class = paste(sort(unique(na.omit(Drug_Class[Drug_Class != ""]))), collapse = "; "),
    Resistance_Mechanism = paste(sort(unique(na.omit(Resistance_Mechanism[Resistance_Mechanism != ""]))), collapse = "; "),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

top20_aro_export <- res_aro_both %>%
  select(
    ARO_Term,
    log2FC_PIG_CTRL,
    pvalue_PIG_CTRL,
    padj_PIG_CTRL,
    log2FC_PIGFARMER_CTRL,
    pvalue_PIGFARMER_CTRL,
    padj_PIGFARMER_CTRL
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    median_log2FC = apply(
      cbind(log2FC_PIG_CTRL, log2FC_PIGFARMER_CTRL),
      1,
      function(x) median(x, na.rm = TRUE)
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(ARO_Term %in% top20_aro_terms) %>%
  left_join(aro_annotation_tbl, by = "ARO_Term") %>%
  mutate(
    Drug_Class = if_else(is.na(Drug_Class) | Drug_Class == "", "unannotated", Drug_Class),
    Resistance_Mechanism = if_else(
      is.na(Resistance_Mechanism) | Resistance_Mechanism == "",
      "unannotated",
      Resistance_Mechanism
    )
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(median_log2FC), desc(log2FC_PIG_CTRL), desc(log2FC_PIGFARMER_CTRL)) %>%
  select(
    ARO_Term,
    Drug_Class,
    Resistance_Mechanism,
    median_log2FC,
    log2FC_PIG_CTRL,
    pvalue_PIG_CTRL,
    padj_PIG_CTRL,
    log2FC_PIGFARMER_CTRL,
    pvalue_PIGFARMER_CTRL,
    padj_PIGFARMER_CTRL
  )

write_csv(top20_aro_export, "../results/top20_aro_terms_deseq2_annotated.csv")

scatterplot_ARO <- ggplot(res_aro_both,
                          aes(x = log2FC_PIG_CTRL, y = log2FC_PIGFARMER_CTRL)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_point(data = res_aro_both %>% filter(ARO_Term %in% top20_aro_terms), colour = "red") +
  geom_text_repel(
    data = res_aro_both %>% filter(ARO_Term %in% top20_aro_terms),
    aes(label = ARO_Term),
    max.overlaps = 50,
    box.padding = 0.3
  ) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(-1, 1), colour = "black", linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = c(-1, 1), colour = "black", linetype = "dashed") +
  theme_bw(base_family = "DejaVu Sans", base_size = FIG_BASE_SIZE) +
  xlab("Log2FC | CTRL < PIG (ARO level)") +
  ylab("Log2FC | CTRL < PIG FARMER (ARO level)")

print(scatterplot_ARO)
ggsave("../figures/ARO_Term_log2FC_scatterplot.svg", scatterplot_ARO, width = 6, height = 6, web_fonts = "DejaVu Sans")
```

```{r}
# Highlight selected ARO terms on the DESeq2 ARO-level log2FC scatterplot
highlight_terms <- c("adeF", "tet(O)")

highlight_points <- res_aro_both %>%
  filter(ARO_Term %in% highlight_terms)

missing_highlight_terms <- setdiff(highlight_terms, highlight_points$ARO_Term)
if (length(missing_highlight_terms) > 0) {
  message("Not found in DESeq2 results: ", paste(missing_highlight_terms, collapse = ", "))
}

p_highlight_adef_teto <- ggplot(res_aro_both, aes(x = log2FC_PIG_CTRL, y = log2FC_PIGFARMER_CTRL)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.35, color = "grey55") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(-1, 1), colour = "black", linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = c(-1, 1), colour = "black", linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_point(data = highlight_points, color = "#D62728", size = 3.2) +
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
    data = highlight_points,
    aes(label = ARO_Term),
    color = "#D62728",
    size = 4,
    box.padding = 0.35,
    point.padding = 0.25,
    max.overlaps = Inf
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_family = "DejaVu Sans", base_size = FIG_BASE_SIZE) +
  labs(
    title = "DESeq2 ARO-term log2FC scatter with highlighted terms",
    subtitle = "Highlighted: adeF and tet(O)",
    x = "Log2FC | CTRL < PIG (ARO level)",
    y = "Log2FC | CTRL < PIG FARMER (ARO level)"
  )

print(p_highlight_adef_teto)
ggsave(
  "../figures/ARO_Term_log2FC_scatterplot_highlight_adeF_tetO.svg",
  p_highlight_adef_teto,
  width = 6,
  height = 6,
  web_fonts = "DejaVu Sans"
)
```

inspect dataframes

```{r}
dim(counts_aro_mat)
dim(meta)

head(colnames(counts_aro_mat), 10)
head(rownames(meta), 10)

# boolean checks
all(colnames(counts_aro_mat) %in% rownames(meta))   # are all sample names present in meta?
all(rownames(meta) %in% colnames(counts_aro_mat))   # are there extra rows in meta?
identical(colnames(counts_aro_mat), rownames(meta)) # exact order + names match?

```

#### Volcano plots and top-25 heatmaps

```{r}
# Volcano plots for both contrasts
plot_volcano <- function(res_tbl, name, alpha = 0.05, lfc_cut = 1) {
  df <- res_tbl %>% mutate(padj = ifelse(is.na(padj), 1, padj))
  df <- df %>% mutate(sig = padj < alpha & abs(log2FoldChange) >= lfc_cut)
  p <- ggplot(df, aes(x = log2FoldChange, y = -log10(padj + 1e-300))) +
    geom_point(aes(color = sig), alpha = 0.6, size = 1.2) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("FALSE" = "grey70", "TRUE" = "red3"), guide = FALSE) +
    theme_minimal() +
    labs(title = name, x = "log2 fold change", y = "-log10(padj)")
  print(p)
  ggsave(file.path("../figures", paste0("volcano_", name, ".svg")), p, width = 6, height = 4)
}

plot_volcano(res_aro_farmer_vs_ctrl, "Pig_farmers_vs_Non_farmer_controls")
plot_volcano(res_aro_pig_vs_ctrl, "Pigs_vs_Non_farmer_controls")

# Top-25 heatmaps from VST-normalized counts
vsd <- tryCatch(
  vst(dds_aro, blind = TRUE),
  error = function(e) {
    message("vst failed: ", conditionMessage(e), " — falling back to varianceStabilizingTransformation()")
    varianceStabilizingTransformation(dds_aro, blind = TRUE)
  }
)

plot_top_heatmap <- function(vsd_obj, res_tbl, name, n_top = 25) {
  genes <- res_tbl %>%
    filter(!is.na(padj)) %>%
    arrange(padj) %>%
    slice_head(n = n_top) %>%
    pull(ARO_Term)

  if (length(genes) < 2) {
    genes <- res_tbl %>%
      arrange(desc(abs(log2FoldChange))) %>%
      slice_head(n = min(n_top, nrow(res_tbl))) %>%
      pull(ARO_Term)
  }

  mat <- assay(vsd_obj)[genes, rownames(meta), drop = FALSE]
  mat_z <- t(scale(t(mat)))
  ann <- data.frame(category = meta$category)
  rownames(ann) <- rownames(meta)

  pheatmap(
    mat_z,
    cluster_rows = TRUE,
    cluster_cols = TRUE,
    annotation_col = ann,
    filename = file.path("../figures", paste0("heatmap_top", length(genes), "_", name, ".png")),
    width = 8,
    height = 6
  )
}

plot_top_heatmap(vsd, res_aro_farmer_vs_ctrl, "Pig_farmers_vs_Non_farmer_controls", n_top = 25)
plot_top_heatmap(vsd, res_aro_pig_vs_ctrl, "Pigs_vs_Non_farmer_controls", n_top = 25)
```



### Richness and Drug-Classes ARO

#### richness
```{r}

# ARO-Term level richness and alpha diversity (depth-corrected)

# 1) Aggregate CPM from gene level to ARO-Term level
gene_to_aro_local <- feats %>%
  rownames_to_column("Prevalence_Sequence_ID") %>%
  transmute(
    Prevalence_Sequence_ID,
    ARO_Term = if_else(
      is.na(ARO_Term) | ARO_Term == "",
      Prevalence_Sequence_ID,
      as.character(ARO_Term)
    )
  )

cpm_aro_long_local <- cpm %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  rownames_to_column("Prevalence_Sequence_ID") %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -Prevalence_Sequence_ID,
    names_to = "sample",
    values_to = "cpm"
  ) %>%
  left_join(gene_to_aro_local, by = "Prevalence_Sequence_ID") %>%
  group_by(sample, ARO_Term) %>%
  summarise(cpm_aro = sum(cpm, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

cpm_aro_mat <- cpm_aro_long_local %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = sample, values_from = cpm_aro, values_fill = 0) %>%
  as.data.frame()

rownames(cpm_aro_mat) <- cpm_aro_mat$ARO_Term
cpm_aro_mat$ARO_Term <- NULL
cpm_aro_mat <- as.matrix(cpm_aro_mat)

# Keep only samples present in metadata and depth vector
samples_keep <- intersect(colnames(cpm_aro_mat), rownames(meta))
samples_keep <- intersect(samples_keep, names(sample_depths))
cpm_aro_mat <- cpm_aro_mat[, samples_keep, drop = FALSE]

# 2) Richness on ARO-Term level (presence/absence of ARO terms)
richness_aro_df <- tibble(
  sample = colnames(cpm_aro_mat),
  aro_richness = colSums(cpm_aro_mat > 0, na.rm = TRUE)
) %>%
  left_join(meta %>% rownames_to_column("sample"), by = "sample") %>%
  mutate(
    category = as.factor(category),
    depth = as.numeric(sample_depths[sample])
  )

# Kruskal-Wallis test for the richness plot
kw_richness_aro <- rstatix::kruskal_test(richness_aro_df, aro_richness ~ category)

# perform a pairwise Wilcoxon-test
pairwise_richness_aro <- richness_aro_df %>%
  pairwise_wilcox_test(aro_richness ~ category, p.adjust.method = "BH") %>%
  add_significance() %>%
  mutate(
    y.position = seq(
      from = max(richness_aro_df$aro_richness, na.rm = TRUE) * 1.05,
      to   = max(richness_aro_df$aro_richness, na.rm = TRUE) * 1.25,
      length.out = n()
    )
  )

# richness plot with global Kruskal-Wallis p-value annotation
p_richness_aro <- ggplot(data = richness_aro_df, aes(x = category, y = aro_richness, fill = category)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.2, alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = sample_type_colors) +
  labs(
    title = "ARO-term richness by category",
    subtitle = "Global p-value from Kruskal-Wallis test",
    x = NULL,
    y = "ARO-term richness"
  ) +
  stat_pvalue_manual(
    pairwise_richness_aro,
    label = "p.adj",
    tip.length = 0.01,
    size = 5,
    family = "DejaVu Sans"
  )+
  scale_x_discrete(labels = color_codes$label_mapping)+
  theme_publication() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")


print(p_richness_aro)

ggsave("../figures/aro_term_richness_by_category_kruskal.svg", p_richness_aro, width = 8, height = 6, web_fonts = "DejaVu Sans")


# Richness on ARO-term level by pig growth stage (Pigs only)
richness_aro_stage_df <- richness_aro_df %>%
  filter(category == "Pigs", !is.na(growth_stage), growth_stage != "") %>%
  mutate(growth_stage = as.character(growth_stage))


ordered_stages <- c("Suckling", "Weaning", "Fattening")
present_stages <- ordered_stages[ordered_stages %in% unique(richness_aro_stage_df$growth_stage)]

if (length(present_stages) >= 2) {
  richness_aro_stage_df <- richness_aro_stage_df %>%
    filter(growth_stage %in% present_stages) %>%
    mutate(growth_stage = factor(growth_stage, levels = present_stages))

  pairwise_richness_aro_stage <- richness_aro_stage_df %>%
    pairwise_wilcox_test(aro_richness ~ growth_stage, p.adjust.method = "BH") %>%
    add_significance() %>%
    mutate(
      y.position = seq(
        from = max(richness_aro_stage_df$aro_richness, na.rm = TRUE) * 1.05,
        to = max(richness_aro_stage_df$aro_richness, na.rm = TRUE) * 1.20,
        length.out = n()
      ),
      label = if_else(p.adj < 0.001, "p < 0.001", paste0("p = ", signif(p.adj, 3)))
    )

  p_richness_aro_stage <- ggplot(
    richness_aro_stage_df,
    aes(x = growth_stage, y = aro_richness, fill = growth_stage)
  ) +
    geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.8) +
    geom_jitter(width = 0.15, alpha = 0.55) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = pig_stage_colors[present_stages], drop = FALSE) +
    labs(
      title = "ARO-term richness by pig growth stage",
      subtitle = "Pairwise Wilcoxon rank-sum tests (BH-adjusted)",
      x = NULL,
      y = "ARO-term richness"
    ) +
    stat_pvalue_manual(
      pairwise_richness_aro_stage,
      label = "label",
      tip.length = 0.01,
      size = 5,
      family = "DejaVu Sans"
    ) +
    theme_publication() +
    theme(legend.position = "none")

  print(p_richness_aro_stage)

  write_csv(pairwise_richness_aro_stage, "../results/aro_term_richness_pig_growth_stage_pairwise_wilcox.csv")

  ggsave(
    "../figures/aro_term_richness_by_pig_growth_stage_wilcox.svg",
    p_richness_aro_stage,
    width = 8,
    height = 6,
    web_fonts = "DejaVu Sans"
  )
} else {
  message("Not enough pig growth stages available for pairwise Wilcoxon tests on ARO-term richness.")
}




```

#### alpha diversity ARO-Term level
```{r}
# alpha diversity 

lm_richness_aro <- lm(aro_richness ~ category + depth, data = richness_aro_df)
anova_richness_aro <- anova(lm_richness_aro)
emm_richness_aro <- emmeans::emmeans(lm_richness_aro, ~ category)
posthoc_richness_aro <- pairs(emm_richness_aro, adjust = "tukey") %>% as.data.frame()

cat("=== ARO-Term richness (depth corrected) ===\n")
print(anova_richness_aro)
cat("Category p-value:", anova_richness_aro["category", "Pr(>F)"], "\n")
print(posthoc_richness_aro)
print(kw_richness_aro)

# 3) Shannon + Simpson on ARO-Term level (depth corrected)
alpha_aro_df <- tibble(
  sample = colnames(cpm_aro_mat),
  Shannon = vegan::diversity(t(cpm_aro_mat), index = "shannon"),
  Simpson = vegan::diversity(t(cpm_aro_mat), index = "simpson")
) %>%
  left_join(meta %>% rownames_to_column("sample"), by = "sample") %>%
  mutate(
    category = as.factor(category),
    depth = as.numeric(sample_depths[sample])
  )

alpha_aro_models <- c("Shannon", "Simpson") %>%
  set_names() %>%
  purrr::map(~ lm(as.formula(paste(.x, "~ category + depth")), data = alpha_aro_df))

alpha_aro_anova <- purrr::map2_dfr(alpha_aro_models, names(alpha_aro_models), ~ {
  a <- anova(.x)
  tibble(
    measure = .y,
    p_category = a["category", "Pr(>F)"],
    p_depth = a["depth", "Pr(>F)"]
  )
})

alpha_aro_posthoc <- purrr::map2_dfr(alpha_aro_models, names(alpha_aro_models), ~ {
  emmeans::emmeans(.x, ~ category) %>%
    pairs(adjust = "tukey") %>%
    as.data.frame() %>%
    mutate(measure = .y)
})

cat("=== ARO-Term alpha diversity (depth corrected) ===\n")
print(alpha_aro_anova)
print(alpha_aro_posthoc)

# alpha diversity plot (depth-corrected statistics from linear model + Tukey)
alpha_aro_posthoc_plot <- alpha_aro_posthoc %>%
  tidyr::separate(contrast, into = c("group1", "group2"), sep = " - ") %>%
  mutate(
    group1 = trimws(group1),
    group2 = trimws(group2)
  ) %>%
  group_by(measure) %>%
  mutate(
    y.position = seq(
      from = max(alpha_aro_df[[measure[1]]], na.rm = TRUE) * 1.03,
      by = diff(range(alpha_aro_df[[measure[1]]], na.rm = TRUE)) * 0.10,
      length.out = n()
    ),
    label = scales::pvalue(p.value, add_p = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

alpha_aro_long <- alpha_aro_df %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c("Shannon", "Simpson"),
    names_to = "measure",
    values_to = "value"
  )

p_alpha_aro <- ggplot(alpha_aro_long, aes(x = category, y = value, fill = category)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.75, outlier.shape = NA) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.18, alpha = 0.55, size = 1.8) +
  stat_pvalue_manual(
    alpha_aro_posthoc_plot,
    label = "label",
    y.position = "y.position",
    xmin = "group1",
    xmax = "group2",
    tip.length = 0.01,
    step.increase = 0,
    bracket.size = 0.35, 
    family = "DejaVu Sans"
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~measure, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = sample_type_colors) +
  labs(
    title = "ARO-Term alpha diversity by category",
    subtitle = "Linear model (depth-corrected) with Tukey-adjusted pairwise p-values",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Diversity index"
  ) +
  theme_publication() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")+
  scale_x_discrete(labels = color_codes$label_mapping)

print(p_alpha_aro)

write_csv(richness_aro_df, "../results/aro_term_richness_depth_corrected_values.csv")
write_csv(posthoc_richness_aro, "../results/aro_term_richness_depth_corrected_posthoc_tukey.csv")
write_csv(kw_richness_aro, "../results/aro_term_richness_kruskal_test.csv")
write_csv(alpha_aro_df, "../results/aro_term_alpha_diversity_depth_corrected_values.csv")
write_csv(alpha_aro_anova, "../results/aro_term_alpha_diversity_depth_corrected_anova.csv")
write_csv(alpha_aro_posthoc, "../results/aro_term_alpha_diversity_depth_corrected_posthoc_tukey.csv")

ggsave("../figures/aro_term_alpha_diversity_depth_corrected.svg", p_alpha_aro, width = 10.5, height = 6, web_fonts = "DejaVu Sans")

```


#### Pileou's eveness
```{r}

# Pielou's evenness on ARO-Term level: J = Shannon / log(S)
evenness_aro_df <- alpha_aro_df %>%
  left_join(richness_aro_df %>% select(sample, aro_richness), by = "sample") %>%
  mutate(
    pielou_J = if_else(aro_richness > 1, Shannon / log(aro_richness), NA_real_),
    category = as.factor(category)
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(pielou_J), is.finite(pielou_J))

# Depth-corrected linear model and Tukey posthoc
lm_evenness_aro <- lm(pielou_J ~ category + depth, data = evenness_aro_df)
anova_evenness_aro <- anova(lm_evenness_aro)
emm_evenness_aro <- emmeans::emmeans(lm_evenness_aro, ~ category)
posthoc_evenness_aro <- pairs(emm_evenness_aro, adjust = "tukey") %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  tidyr::separate(contrast, into = c("group1", "group2"), sep = " - ") %>%
  mutate(
    group1 = trimws(group1),
    group2 = trimws(group2),
    label = scales::pvalue(p.value, add_p = TRUE)
  )

posthoc_evenness_aro <- posthoc_evenness_aro %>%
  mutate(
    y.position = seq(
      from = max(evenness_aro_df$pielou_J, na.rm = TRUE) * 1.03,
      by = diff(range(evenness_aro_df$pielou_J, na.rm = TRUE)) * 0.10,
      length.out = n()
    )
  )

p_evenness_aro <- ggplot(evenness_aro_df, aes(x = category, y = pielou_J, fill = category)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, outlier.shape = NA) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.18, alpha = 0.55, size = 1.8) +
  stat_pvalue_manual(
    posthoc_evenness_aro,
    label = "label",
    y.position = "y.position",
    xmin = "group1",
    xmax = "group2",
    tip.length = 0.01,
    bracket.size = 0.35,
    family = "DejaVu Sans"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = sample_type_colors) +
  labs(
    title = "Pielou's Evenness (J) on ARO-Term level",
    subtitle = "Linear model (depth-corrected) with Tukey-adjusted pairwise p-values",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Evenness index (J)"
  ) +
  theme_publication() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")+
  scale_x_discrete(labels = color_codes$label_mapping)

print(p_evenness_aro)

cat("=== ARO-Term Pielou evenness (depth corrected) ===\n")
print(anova_evenness_aro)
print(posthoc_evenness_aro)

write_csv(evenness_aro_df, "../results/aro_term_pielou_evenness_depth_corrected_values.csv")
write_csv(
  tibble(
    measure = "Pielou_J",
    p_category = anova_evenness_aro["category", "Pr(>F)"],
    p_depth = anova_evenness_aro["depth", "Pr(>F)"]
  ),
  "../results/aro_term_pielou_evenness_depth_corrected_anova.csv"
)
write_csv(posthoc_evenness_aro, "../results/aro_term_pielou_evenness_depth_corrected_posthoc_tukey.csv")

ggsave(
  "../figures/aro_term_pielou_evenness_depth_corrected.svg",
  p_evenness_aro,
  width = 6,
  height = 6,
  web_fonts = "DejaVu Sans"
)

```


## CARD - Drug-Classes 
Aggregate `CPM` first to `ARO_Term`, then to `Drug_Class` per sample, and plot abundance by sample category.

```{r}
# Build a gene -> ARO map
gene_to_aro <- feats %>%
  rownames_to_column("Prevalence_Sequence_ID") %>%
  transmute(
    Prevalence_Sequence_ID,
    ARO_Term = if_else(is.na(ARO_Term) | ARO_Term == "", Prevalence_Sequence_ID, ARO_Term)
  )

# CPM matrix to long format (gene x sample)
cpm_long <- cpm %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  rownames_to_column("Prevalence_Sequence_ID") %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -Prevalence_Sequence_ID,
    names_to = "sample",
    values_to = "cpm"
  )

# 1) Aggregate to ARO_Term per sample
cpm_aro_long <- cpm_long %>%
  left_join(gene_to_aro, by = "Prevalence_Sequence_ID") %>%
  group_by(sample, ARO_Term) %>%
  summarise(cpm_aro = sum(cpm, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

# 2) Map ARO_Term -> Drug_Class (split multi-classes and weight equally)
aro_to_drug <- feats %>%
  rownames_to_column("Prevalence_Sequence_ID") %>%
  transmute(
    ARO_Term = if_else(is.na(ARO_Term) | ARO_Term == "", Prevalence_Sequence_ID, ARO_Term),
    Drug_Class
  ) %>%
  distinct() %>%
  mutate(Drug_Class = str_split(Drug_Class, ";\\s*")) %>%
  unnest(Drug_Class) %>%
  mutate(Drug_Class = str_trim(Drug_Class)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Drug_Class), Drug_Class != "") %>%
  distinct() %>%
  group_by(ARO_Term) %>%
  mutate(n_drug_classes = n(), weight = 1 / n_drug_classes) %>%
  ungroup()

# 3) Aggregate to Drug_Class per sample
cpm_drugclass_long <- cpm_aro_long %>%
  left_join(aro_to_drug, by = "ARO_Term") %>%
  filter(!is.na(Drug_Class)) %>%
  mutate(cpm_weighted = cpm_aro * weight) %>%
  group_by(sample, Drug_Class) %>%
  summarise(cpm_drugclass = sum(cpm_weighted, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  left_join(
    meta %>%
      rownames_to_column("sample") %>%
      select(sample, category),
    by = "sample"
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(category))

# Save aggregated table for downstream analyses
write_csv(cpm_drugclass_long, "../results/cpm_drugclass_per_sample.csv")

cat("Rows in cpm_drugclass_long:", nrow(cpm_drugclass_long), "\n")
cat("Unique drug classes:", n_distinct(cpm_drugclass_long$Drug_Class), "\n")
```


### extract names of CARD drug classes

```{r}
aro_meta_long <- feats %>%
  select(ARO_Term, Drug_Class, Resistance_Mechanism) %>%
  distinct() %>%
  # split semicolon-separated fields into multiple rows
  mutate(
    Drug_Class = str_split(Drug_Class, ";\\s*"),
    Resistance_Mechanism = str_split(Resistance_Mechanism, ";\\s*")
  ) %>%
  unnest(Drug_Class) %>%
  unnest(Resistance_Mechanism) %>%
  mutate(
    Drug_Class = str_trim(Drug_Class),
    Resistance_Mechanism = str_trim(Resistance_Mechanism)
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Drug_Class) & !is.na(Resistance_Mechanism) & 
         Drug_Class != "" & Resistance_Mechanism != "") %>%
  distinct()

unique(aro_meta_long$Drug_Class)
```
### Drug Class mean abundance
this should give a table, where we have the drug class and then the mean abundance per drug class from the cpm values over all samples.
Since I want to make a new annotation of the drug classes it's important to then be able to set a threshold in order to validate the new drug class names
```{r}
drug_class_mean_cpm <- cpm_drugclass_long %>%
  group_by(Drug_Class)%>%
  summarise(
    mean_drug_class_cpm = mean(cpm_drugclass)
  )%>%
  ungroup()%>%
  arrange(desc(mean_drug_class_cpm))

head(drug_class_mean_cpm)


ggplot(data = drug_class_mean_cpm, aes (x = mean_drug_class_cpm))+
  geom_histogram()

write_csv(drug_class_mean_cpm, "../results/drug_class_mean_cpm.csv")





```

### relative abundance barplot per category

```{r}
# Relative Abundanz pro Sample und Drug Class
# Order classes by overall abundance for consistent stack + legend order.
drug_class_levels <- cpm_drugclass_long %>%
  group_by(Drug_Class) %>%
  summarise(total_cpm = sum(cpm_drugclass, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(desc(total_cpm)) %>%
  pull(Drug_Class)

drug_class_palette <- grDevices::hcl.colors(length(drug_class_levels), palette = "Dynamic")
names(drug_class_palette) <- drug_class_levels

# Keep "others" neutral if present.
other_idx <- which(tolower(names(drug_class_palette)) == "others")
if (length(other_idx) > 0) {
  drug_class_palette[other_idx] <- "#BDBDBD"
}

plot_rel_abundance_drugclass <- cpm_drugclass_long %>%
  mutate(Drug_Class = factor(Drug_Class, levels = drug_class_levels)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = category, y = cpm_drugclass, fill = Drug_Class)) +
  geom_col(position = "fill") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = drug_class_palette, drop = FALSE) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Relative abundance", fill = "Drug class") +
  my_theme()

print(plot_rel_abundance_drugclass)

# ggsave
ggsave("../figures/drug_class_rel_abundances.svg", plot = plot_rel_abundance_drugclass, width = 12, height = 12, web_fonts = "DejaVu Sans")
```

## Final drug class annotation

### load manual annotation

```{r}
library(readxl)
drug_class_annot_manual <- read_excel("../drug_class_fam_mean_cpm_2.xlsx")

glimpse(drug_class_annot_manual)

length(unique(drug_class_annot_manual$Drug_Class_Resfinder))
```
